---
title: "Investment Portfolio Management"
subtitle: "6 week investmentment challenge"
descritpion: ""
categories: [Finance, Year 3]
image: "/projects/portfolio_management/investment_portfolio.jpg"
date: '2025-07-04'
author: 
    - "Martinas Jucys Brady"

about:
  template: solana
  links:
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: Investment Portfolio XLSX 
      href: projects/portfolio_management/investment_portfolio.xlsx
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: Portfolio Presentation PDF
      href: projects/portfolio_management/portfolio_presentation.pdf
---

![Cover Photo](/projects/portfolio_management/investment_portfolio.jpg){width="80%"}

---

## Initial Portfolio

For my initial portfolio my main target was to diversify assets and minimalise any correlation that exists. I started off by choosing 32 assets from varying sectors and industries and took their stock price on a quarterly basis for 5 years starting from 19th May 2025, as I feel like this is more appropriate to meet a balance between the overall portfolio which is due in 2 months and a portfolio used for long term gains. Through more research I began to remove certain assets that had high correlation with multiple other assets and also those that I did not see as a worthwhile risk.

A few issues I ran into were adapting the data to include companies that were less than 5 years old, and that I had to adjust the risk-free rate in order to find the proper Sharpe Ratio.

These are the asset classes and their respective portfolio weight -

- Bonds	15% ( BNDX was included to reduce volatility and have a “stable” growth.)

- ETF 25% (VOO is the largest holding for this asset class as it allows direct access to the 500 largest companies in the US – which is heavily weighted by companies within the Technology sector.)

- Stocks	 30% (This asset class includes a variety of different assets from different sectors, I decided to not include any stocks from companies whose primary sector is Technology as I feel that the inclusion of VOO already gives enough exposure to this uncertain market. I also decided against including the likes of JNJ and PFE due to the current uncertainty with the U.S.’s policies over the Healthcare industry.)

- Cryptocurrency 6% ( Although I avoided some risks within the market at the moment, and that BTC does have an extremely high volatility, I did decide to allocate a small percentage of my portfolio to cryptocurrency – BTC in particular – as a high-risk high-reward asset.)

- REIT 10% ( similarly to BNDX, REITs can protect the portfolio against inflation but there is an extra amount of risk involved as REITs can be more volatile, and changes can happen very quickly.)

- Cash ($, £, €) 14% (In case of any sudden future opportunities and to maintain a liquid asset. Small exposure to the EUR and GBP as some assets within the portfolio are traded in these currencies and relatively low risk.)

I’m not fully sure about the allocations at the moment, Sharpe Ratio of 0.61 indicates that a lot of improvement can be made. When I’ve used the solve function in Excel I’ve noticed that it tends to heavily weigh a portfolio on 1-2 assets so I’ve had to experiment a lot with the function and try to give sensible allocations to specific assets.

I selected my initial shortlist of assets by a mix of random and personal choices, and with the aim of having exposure to a large amount of sectors.

To narrow down this shortlist of assets I then made a correlation matrix and removed assets that were highly correlated with others (for example, I found that companies such as Amazon and Nvidia has very high correlation with other assets). I also tried to ensure that certain sectors were not too heavily weighted in the portfolio (hence I avoided using securities within the Technology sector as VOO is already heavily dominated by the technology sector and gives me sufficient exposure - I calculated that my portfolio is still ~22% weighted by technology sector securities right now).

I also decided that it may be best not to include some stocks as there was simply not enough information about them, or they are present in sectors that are currently going through a lot of change within the U.S. I also included some assets like BTC to ensure that I at least have a small exposure to the cryptocurrency market if it were to gain more traction (possibly if the U.S. were to implement more open usage of it - unlikely but at this moment it seems like anything could be possible due to the heightened unpredictability of the market).

Regarding my risk and reward objectives, my realistic goal would be for annualised returns of ~8%, and maintaining standard deviation of below 10%. In my roughwork and calculation for the Variance-Covariance Matrix I used Quarterly data and not annual. 

From my understanding the Sharpe ratio will change by quite a bit if I were to change this data into Annual data. From using solver I noticed that it would give very unrealistic allocations to high volatility stocks that I personally wouldn't be willing to risk so much on (e.g. TSLA, NVDA), so I had to adjust parts of the Solver function and set realistic limits as to how much I would put into each Asset class and sector.

My current Sharpe ratio based on Quarterly calculations is 0.61, but now I have tested it using Annual and have received a Sharpe ratio of 1.34, Expected Return of 17.81% and a Standard Deviation of 12.58%. From this I gather that I am exceeding my initial objectives for SR and ER but not SD.

From revising over my portfolio it may be that BTC is too heavily weighted and I may need to lower the portfolio weight of it.

## Week 1

*My initial portfolio was based on Monday closing prices, Monday the 26th of May was memorial day so markets were closed.Therefore, I used the closing price of Tuesday the 27th of May.*

- Portfolio Value - $101,009.45 (+1.01% gain)

- Top Performer  

(%) RHM.DE - Rheinmetal AG (+9.58% gain)

($) - RHM.DE - Rheinmetal AG (+$564.27 gain)

- Worst Performer 

(%) - PLD - Prologis (-3.64% loss)

($) - PLD - Prologis (-$290.16 loss)   

I've noticed that both Sharpe ratios (Annual and Quarterly) have increased by 0.02, meaning my portfolio has improved itself. Action still needs to be taken regarding the reduction of the standard deviation, so as I briefly mentioned  at the end of the initial portfolio review, I will need to lower the weight of BTC. I will also increase investment into the BNDX bond.

The following are the new sharpe ratios after the sale of 0.01 BTC and purchase of 15 BNDX. We see here that sd has been decreased, but sharpe ratio has went down by 0.01 in the quarterly data and up by 0.01 in the annual data.

Using quarterly data:

Sum of weight - 1
Portfolio Expected return - 4.02% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation   4.69%         
Expected Sharp ratio   0.63 

Using annual data:

Sum of weight   0.96247 
Portfolio Expected return 18.04% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 12.35%         
Expected Sharp ratio   1.37 

During the week ahead I will monitor the European market (Financials has caught my eye - BNP.PA, SAN.MC, PKO.WA, HSBA.L, EUFN especially) closely as I see a possibility of further growth here as trade tensions with the US seem to grow further, and the EU look to strengthen their own defence more. So far, I have the Industrials sector of Europe represented, so I should take a look at more sectors within the European market to include to ensure appropriate diversification. 

Although TSLA is notoriously volatile I trusted my insightful thoughts in including this asset in my portfolio. Elon Musk stepping down from his role in the US government and "giving more of his time to the growth of Tesla" has predictably increased investor interest.

Financial Services and REITs are my highest losing sectors, this seems to be from the overall market uncertainty in the US.

The US based energy assets (XOM and SLNG) have also seen a decline, primarily due to oil price decreases, while the Norwegian based asset (EQNR) has grown by >5%. It seems that ENQR was given the green light to proceed with a large wind farm project for the EU. This further strengthens my idea that I may need to diversify further into the EU market.

All other areas covered in my portfolio have seemed to have remained stable with minimal movement.

Along with the European financials sector I will be monitoring the European Consumer sector (ULVR.L, DGE.L, RKT.L, LVMH.PA, OR.PA to name a few that I will consider).

Both the EUR and GBP have strengthened slightly compared to the USD, giving >1% growth in each.

Moving forward I realise that further diversifcation is needed, while also being mindful of the effect of adding too many variables on the er, sd and sharpe ratio of my portfolio. I also need to be aware that even though right now the US market may look somewhat uncertain and at risk of suffering from increased volatility, the European market can also change very quickly. Diversification between sectors and markets is important but I must not overexpose myself to areas of the market like industrials, crypto, reits and technology. 

Future research may need to be done to identify if there is any investment opportunities in Asian countries, extra caution must be taken as many economies are heavily influenced by political events (action taken by China or in-country political issues).

*Notable Events*

- On May 23rd the US first threatened the EU with a 50% tariff on June 1, but through tough discussions during the weekend the US delayed the 50% tariff on the EU until July 9. This caused much of US based assets to drop by ~1% on the 23rd of May.

- Also on May 23rd the US threated a 25% tariff on iPhones manufactured outside of the US, this caused AAPL to fall by ~3% promptly.

- Markets were closed on Monday due to Memorial Day.

- On May 27, markets reopened and surged after the news of the tariff delay.

*Further points*

- My target risk and return are the same as the overview of my initial portfolio, where my realistic goal would be of ~8% annualised return and a standard deviation of below 10%. 

- So originally I used quarterly figures to see more accurate short-term stock data, as this assignments is based over ~2 months performance, where now I think that using returns and sd from this is ok, the Sharpe ratio is less relevant from the quarterly data. The use of quarterly data also lets me see the extent of correlation much more accurately when it comes to assets, one of my original goals was to minimise correlation.

- So yes I plan to adjust my own portfolio slowly over the coming weeks (big changes at once would not be beneficial), this week of monitoring my portfolio should allow me to get a more rounded view as to which assets I can reduce my allocation to and areas where I should increase allocation. So far, as per the transactions sheet, I have sold ~15% of my BTC shares and added more bond exposure through BNDX. This was done as in the initial portfolio overview I made the observation that BTC share should be lowered in order to help meet my goal of a <10% standard deviation. 

- I will also be acting upon my observation that further diversifying into other markets (European) may be necessary to ensure that losses due to uncertainty within the US market may incur on my portfolio. Minimal correlation, <10% sd and maintaining a annualised return of above or ~8% is the main objective. As we can see with my diversification of the Energy sector (EQNR, SLNG and XOM), the American based assets performed negatively while the European based asset performed positively due to market fluctuations regarding oil and green energy. 

## Week 2

- Portfolio Value - $100,495.07 (-0.51%)

- Top Performer     

(%) - PLD - Prologis (+2.00% gain)

($) - PLD - Prologis (+153.36 gain)

- Worst Performer 

(%) - TSLA - Tesla, Inc. (-5.57% loss)

($) - RHM.DE - Rheinmetal AG (-$258.20 loss)

*Notable Events*

- On May 28th, NVDA strong earnings call, strong boost for the technology sector.

- June 1st, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia caused oil prices to rise.

- June 1st, Bulgaria announced that it will join the eurozone.

- June 2nd, Nawrocki elected president of Poland (right wing, anti EU), has led me to dismiss interest in any Eastern European based assets as negative media attention.

- Further uncertainty over trade tariffs and various sectors in the US markets.

*Effect on Portfolio*

- Overall very little movement in my portfolio.

- Boost from NVDA most likely is what made VOO finish this week on a positive.

- BYD overtook TSLA in being the worlds largest EV maker on the 26/05/2025.

- Increased political and military tensions in many regions of the world isn't helping VWO ETF to grow as it should.

- But, these increased tensions are allowing Industrials sector to grow (weapons and machinery is essential, especially in the EU).


As mentioned last week I've decided to further diversify my portfolio using European based assets, certain assets were removed from my selection due to political reasons (Polish assets), high correlation with other assets (SAN.MC, etc.).


Will be aiming to get closer to my target standard deviation of <10%, while maintaing a high but realistic return and sharpe ratio.

American steel industry may be one to keep a closer eye on, with tariffs being an uncertainty, it is still important to note that Trump is encouraging "Made in the USA". Steel is one of the most important materials in the world so it could be assumed that with or without tariffs, the US steel industry should grow.

*Transactions*

- Adding BNP.PA (European Financial Sector), ULVR.L, DGE.L, RKT.L  (all 3 are part of the European Consumer Sector) in order to diversify the portfolio.

- In order to acheive my target standard deviation of ~10%, from the Solver function I can see that I need to sell the vast majority of the assets GOOGL and TSLA.

- Lowering holdings of BTC again this week in order to avoid large volatility.


Using annual data:

Sum of weight 0.98 
Portfolio Expected return 18.72% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 12.99%     
Expected Sharp ratio  1.36 

Compared to the previous week we can see that our expected return and sd has increased and sharpe ratio in turn has decreased, the changes I have made to the allocation this week should hopefully clearly show the improvement in the three values.


After conducting the transactions, the portfolio looks like this.

Sum of weight   0.97782 
Portfolio Expected return 15.11% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 9.27%         
Expected Sharp ratio   1.17 

From this we can see sd has a value which matches our realistic goal of being below 10%.

The only issue now is that our er has also declined slightly, and so has the most important value of Sharpe ratio.

Using Solver I was able to increase the Sharpe Ratio to 1.43, but this making adjustments that I deemed unnecessary (reducing bond exposure, VOO holdings and increasing weights of one stock to >10%).

Bond exposure is necessary to remove a lot of volatility from the portfolio,

VOO is essential in my portfolio as it gives exposure to sectors and companies that we haven't invested in.

Increasing the weight of one stock to >10% is unappealing as it is the stock market and nothing is truly predictable.


Main focus for improvement when reevaluating next week should be to now increase expected returns, while also maintaining an sd of under 10%.

## Week 3

- Portfolio Value - $102,092.3 (+1.59%)

- Top Performer    

(%) - SBUX - Starbucks Corporation (+6.43% gain)

($) - SBUX - Starbucks Corporation (+$383.60 gain)

- Worst Performer 

(%) - SLNG - Stabilis Solutions, Inc. (-3.97% loss)

($) - RHM.DE - Rheinmetal AG (-$76.41 loss)

*Notable Events*

- June 6th, LA Protests.

- During the week Elon Musk made many comments on "X" regarding Donald Trump. Had a negative impact on TSLA, but has since began recovering.

- Tensions in Europe are increasing between the EU, Ukraine and Russia. 

- US-China trade talks take place in the near future
Effect on Portfolio
Overall a lot of growth, RHM.DE and SLNG are the only heavy losers.


Although RHM.DE has showed our biggest loss this week. We see that the overall EU industrials & defense sector has grown (seen through the NATO.L). This indicates that interest is growing within this market with investors looking to diversify within, it also possibly indicates that RHM.DE was overbough over the previous weeks in comparison to its competitors, so there is better value elsewhere.

Consumer Cyclical sectors has seen growth(SBUX), stagnation(NKE) and decline(TSLA). Increased expected consumer spending within the retail sector, uncertainty over Chinese supply chains, and Elon Musk's comments about Donald Trump have led to each of these changes respectively.

Bitcoin has shown signs of further positive growth as some investors see an opportunity cost in buying BTC due to low forecasted interest rates in the US. Nonetheless it is a volatile asset, but we also see more rumours and news circulating that various governments across the world are considering holding reserves in BTC.

The energy sector seen positive growth, which is heavily reliant on the price of oil. EQNR is still attracting investments from its previous wind farm contracts. XOM is increasing in price in tandem to the market as a whole, with oil prices being a key player. SLNG on the other hand has seemed to be underperforming as of late, small cap companies, especially in the energy sector are very sensitive to market news. Investors are more attracted to companies that are treneding or who have more stability within the market. I will reconsider my holdings of SLNG over the next week.

After selling shares in GOOGL it performed well over the last week, with growth of ~3%., which is in line with other companies in this sector and those of the Technology sector. GOOGL has also been following similar growth patterns to NVDA over the last month, indicating that the growth potential behind technology and AI driven stocks may need to be considered. Must keep high awareness as this market can be volatile.

Financial sector growth has indicated that investors have begun feeling more secure investing in the US market due to news that issues with tariffs will be resolved in the near future, solidifying the safety of investing in the US stock market (BAC and V). BNP.PA also grew at a similar rate indicating that investors are being appealed by the EU market, whcih could be because tariffs on the EU were halted for another month recently. The US showing willingness to negotiate trade deals with countries has led to positive outlooks on investing in foreign markets.

European Consumer Defensive sector has also grown slightly, mainly due to the strengthening of the Euro and Pound.

REITs have stabilised from previous decline and then growth, mainly due to increased stabilisation of the US market as a whole. Although, if the LA riots begin to influence the rest of the US it could bring forward some losses as these REITs (PLD and VICI) rely on market stability. 

*Transactions*

Underperformance of RHM.DE - selling 0.4 shares 

SBUX possibly overperforming - selling 30 shares

Brings the cash weight back to ~10% which is the ideal number for this portfolio.


Sum of weight   0.97703
Portfolio Expected return 14.47% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 9.29%         
Expected Sharp ratio   1.10


Sharpe ratio has decreased further from these transactions, I do feel as though these were logical trades that were needed to be done, holding RHM.DE after high returns in week 1 led me to miss out on profits that many gained from selling when it hit a high price. Learning from this I am doing this with SBUX. The rest of the portfolio has grown slightly so there should be minimal worry to selling.

From the Quarterly data we see that the expected return is 3.47% over 3 months. The portfolio has already grown 2.2% after 3 weeks indicating that it could outperform this if the portfolio keeps at this pace.


VOO is up ~0.8% over this same period of time, indicating that the portfolio is currently doing well, the previous transactions that were made were benefiical in maintaining growth. 


Also needs to be noted that for the Sharpe Ratios, only 5 years of data is being used, if more data were to be used we would get a more accurate values in the Sharpe ratio, do even though a value of 1.10 may not be ideal. Increasing  the Sharpe ratio may cause the portfolio to work less efficiently. If no transactions would have taken place the portfolio would be up a little over 1%.

My target standard deviation is met and annualised expected return has been exceeded. Minimal correlation between assets has been met, ideally I would diversify into more markets on an international level, but larger datasets would be needed. 

## Week 4

- Portfolio Value - $102,512.62 (+0.41%)

- Top Performer     

(%) - EQNR - Equinor ASA (+11.82%)

($) - XOM - Exxon Mobil Corporation (+$337.95)

- Worst Performer 

(%) - SLNG - Stabilis Solutions, Inc. (-9.02%)

($) - PLD - Prologis - (-$151.92)

*Notable Events*

- Iran Israel war has begun, oil prices increased by >10%.

- US China trade talks were held and positive agreements were made for trade in certain sectors.

- Market was dominated by movement in the technology and energy sectors.

*Effect on Portfolio*

EQNR and XOM both benefitted greatly from the tensions in the Middle East, due to more interest in renewable energy and oil prices respectively. This same effect was not seen in SLNG though, being the worst performer in the portfolio by %, due to them being a relatively smaller company. This sensitivity of smaller companies to the news can be clearly seen here, although I do believe that SLNG may now be somewhat undervalued.

BAC and V, both associated with the financial market, were both down this week, possibly due to increased strain on the US economy and overall uncertainty regarding the decisions made on the world platform by the US. Converesely BNP.PA grew ever so slightly, indicating that diversifying into the European finanicial sector could be more beneficial.

BTC lowered in value this week but this can be expected from such a volatile asset after coming from a high of >$110,000, more businesses are showing willingness to commit to BTC purchasing schemes which indicates that more potential growth could be on the horizon.

No other asset class has really faced much movement during this week.

EUR and GBP have been continuously strengthening against the USD.

Possible expectations of growth in stocks and ETFs associated with defense and machinery, if the war escalates further. But must keep aware that with the US cutting a lot of military spending recently, US based companies might not feel the same growth as EU based ones. 

No transactions made this week, with overall satisfaction of the portfolio. maintaining an average ~0.6% growth every week.

Observations must be made about the war in the middle east and involvement from other nations. Increased uncertainty in many US-based assets


Sum of weight 0.98 
Portfolio Expected return 14.44% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 9.04%     
Expected Sharp ratio 1.13

Increased sharpe ratio indicates that the portfolio is moving positively.

## Week 5

- Portfolio Value - $101,618.67 (-0.87%)

- Top Performer     

(%) - TSLA - Tesla, Inc. (+5.94%)

($) - BAC - Bank of America Corporation (+$62.37)

- Worst Performer 

(%) - GOOGL - Alphabet Inc.. (-6.55%)

($) - BNP.PA - BNP Paribas SA - (-$275.80)

*Notable Events*

- The main thing that happened this week was the escalation of war in the Middle East, at the beginning of this week the war begun and oil and gold prices increased, but by the end of the week a ceasefire seemingly has taken place. This caused oil and gold prices to fall back down. With the Middle East being unstable, and the involvement of USA, there has been some sceptisim in the US stock market. 

- More countries and companies are beginning to set plans for holding BTC reserves, which shows that there could be great long term future returns for this asset.

*Effect on Portfolio*

This week has been the worst performance so far.

Tech stocks overall heavily underperformed this week.

With uncertainty with how long this war in the Middle East will actually last, the enrgy sector seen a loss this week.

BAC was one of the only stocks that had a good performance this week, largely due to the easing of tensions and possible end to the war in the Middle East.

V and GOOGL both have some influence in the tech industry so they both felt a loss, possibly from this.

V is also driven by consumer spending and this week theres been a seemingly downward turn on consumer expenditure.

Overall anything linked to technology and defense felt a negative effect.


After the lecture this week I became more aware that an over exposure to any one asset can be negative if continuous portfolio growth is wanted. Visually looking at the portfolio, assets based on the consumer market may not have enough exposure. PLD may be overexposed. American financial assets may be underexposed.

From the lecture I also gathered that exposure to niche markets like the European defense industry is quite beneficial as it provides a platform for stable growth.


Many of the assets in my portfolio have their earnings call coming up within the next month, which will surely lead to heavy fluctuation. The first asset that has an earnings call is NKE. NKE has seemingly hit a slump as of late when looking at the stock price, but it is still one of the most influential brands that exists today, so its presence will most likely not disappear. NKE stock historically has a very strong reaction to earnings calls, whether that be good or bad.


I've also realised the additions that I made to the portfolio a few weeks ago seem to have negatively affected the portfolio hugely with heavy losses. 


I considered shorting BTC, but fees for cryptocurrency transfers are quite high in real life so I feel that would be too much of a shortcut to take.


The sharpe ratio will be heavily effected with the increased allocation in any of the sectors that I want to increase allocation to.

Sharpe Ratio before :

Sum of weight   0.97658                             
Portfolio Expected return 14.38% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 9.08%         
Expected Sharp ratio   1.12 

Sharpe Ratio after :

Sum of weight   0.97740                               
Portfolio Expected return 16.03% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 10.93%         
Expected Sharp ratio   1.08 

*Transactions* 

The basis of these transactions comes from the removal of overweighted assets that have been performing negatively (PLD), and the creation of a more balanced portfolio. The only issue now is that having a 40% weight on stocks is quite risky. The standard deviation sits at just under 11% and the Sharpe ratio has decreased to 1.08.

Sold PLD - underperformance.

Bought NKE, V, RR.L, SBUX and VOO. 

The addition of more VOO is to balance the portfolio more on a more stable asset.

NKE, V, RR.L and SBUX are all added as there is potential for growth in each case. RR.L through new contracts with the UK and EU for defense. NKE, V and SBUX have the potential of growth through more consumer expenditure. NKE has the potential to drop further or increase its stock price, this is heavily depending on the result of their earnings call. 


The addition of more European defense based stocks might have put too much of a weight on this specific sector, but there is a lot of potential for growth here. 

## Week 6

- Portfolio Value -  $104,042.21 (+2.38%)

- Top Performer     

(%) -  NKE - Nike, Inc. - (+16.88%)

($) - RR.L - Rolls-Royce Holdings plc - (+$564.30)

- Worst Performer 

(%) - TSLA - Tesla, Inc. - (-8.9%)

($) - XOM - Exxon Mobil Corporation (-$177.30)

*Notable Events*

- June 24th, NATO countries signed a declaration to reduce dependance on the US for defense purposes. And to allocate 5% of their GDP to defense expenditure by 2035.

- Canada and the US seemingly hit a negotiation roadblock when Canda would not remove their digital services tax, but this has since tax has since suspended and trade talks resumed.

- Tensions between the US and Japan are on the up, with trade talks not going anywhere between auto taxes on Japanese cars, and the Japanese PM did not attend the NATO summit due to disapproval over the push for 5% of GDP allocated to defense expenditure.

- TSLA stocks plummet due to Trump's threats over cutting subisidies to companies run by Musk.

- Top wall street analysts have upgraded NKE to Buy from Hold (as of 1st of July), due to the companies' new focus on e-commerce that will improve pricing.

*Effect on Portfolio*

- All assets except consumer stocks(except NKE), energy stocks and REITs greatly improved this week. The opposite to last weeks scare where we faced the largest losses.

- NKE recorded a very positive upturn due to stronger than expected financials and a promising turnaround plan for their recent slump. The acknowledgement of change needing to be made throughout the business was seen in very good light by investors.

- REITs keep decreasing in value due to the constant uncertainty within this sector regarding regulations. PLD specifically is suffering due to large scale investor uncertainty.

- With geopolitical tensions easing across the world and specifically the Middle East we see oil prices decreasing again, this has a knock on effect on companies within the energy sector.

- Financials and Defense sectors have felt great increases during this week due to these easing worldwide tensions. Investors have become more confident with banks. The defense sector as a whole has benefitted greatly due to new defense agreements by NATO. 

- As usual the market was led by the growth in the AI and technology industry.


Sum of weight 0.98 
Portfolio Expected return 16.53% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 11.40%     
Expected Sharp ratio 1.08 

## Final Evaluation

- Portfolio Value -  $105,053.26 (+0.97%)

- Top Performer     

(%) -  NKE - Nike, Inc. - (+7.53%)

($) - VOO - Vanguard S&P 500 ETF - (+$251.65)

- Worst Performer 

(%) - SLNG - Stabilis Solutions, Inc. - (-6.95%) 

($) - RHM.DE - Rheinmetal AG - (+$104.74)

*Notable Events*

- S&P500 hit all time highs

- US continues to progress with trade deals with various countries (Vietnam, Canada)

- Ukraine and US defense discussions scheduled for next week amid heightened Russian attacks

- Incoming tariffs of up to 70% from the US to countries that don't cooperate

- Many Eastern European countries bordering anti-mining treaty, as they prepare to mine their borders with Russian amid safety concerns.

- Big Beautiful Bill got passed

*Effect on Portfolio*

NKE is still receiving big gains from last weeks successful shareholder meeting, this beign the only significant outlier in the portfolio.

All assets associated with the European Defense market went down slightly, largely due to very good performance previously and noww investors are profit taking. 

All other assets performed quite similarly to the market as a whole.

*Overview on Asset Performance*

Starting with the assets that led to overall losses we see that :

- TSLA:  experienced great volatility over the course of the 6 weeks mainly due to Musk's instability and unpredicatble actions, good short term action by selling 75% of shares in Week 3, but in the long term it does have potential to grow if the right developments are made.  

- SLNG: the largest % loser at more than -20%. Continuous weight of 0.25%, made sure that the risk of investing in a small cap company was not large. 

- DGE.L: Was purchased halfway through the portfolio. DGE.L largely fell a lot due to loss of revenue due to trade disruptions with the US. Especially in its alcohol trade. With easing tensions I feel that this company will recover slowly as seen in the growth this week.

- ULVR.L: similarly to DGE.L trade disruptions largely affected the price of this stock, but it did not perform as bad as DGE.L.

- PLD: Largest $ loser. Underperformed greatly and the economic and geopolitical uncertainty did not help its case. The previous ~7% weight that I had on this REIT was quite high. The Sharpe ratio had largely led to me include 10% weight on REITs but i think that my new overall 5% allocation to these seem much more appropriate, especially with the current volatility in the market.

The majority of the assets included returned a profit :

- VOO gave the portfolio the highest $ gain, largely due to its constant weight of ~18% of the portfolio.  

- GOOGL: solid gains of ~9% were largely due to progress being made in the AI and technology field. Was largely helped by the constant growth of the technology industry.

- SBUX: strong investor confidence and consumer expenditure led to gains of >9%. 

- NKE: massive gain of >25% largely due to last weeks earnings call and increased investor interest in this asset due to a new positive path outlined for the future. The risk of buying more stock before the earnings call proved to be largely positive. This was the best performing asset by % return.

- XOM: growth of just over 5%, largely due to the constant fluctuation in oil prices.

- EQNR: at week 4 it had an overall return of over 20% due to new contracts being given by the EU to EQNR to produce more sustainable energy. Now the overall return sits at ~11%, largely due to the sudden rise in oil prices and then drop again after a cease fire was said to be agreed between Israel and Iran. Investor optimism in the energy market was given a scare overall, with many investors waiting for more stable geopolitical circumstances to invest once again in this sector.

- BAC: experienced most of its growth during the last 3 weeks, where interest rates experienced an increase which benefits the financial institutions. Did not perform that well in the beginning due to economic and geopolitical uncertainty but now this has become more stable and investors feel more confident investing in such.

- V: strong revenues, consumer expenditure and projects in the AI sector have led to them experiencing modest 3% gains.

- RHM.DE, NATO.L (ETF for European based defense assets) and RR.L all had % returns of between 5% and 15% due to the increase in European based defense expenditure due to the agreements made to be less reliant on American defense and machinery. European based defense assets have much more room for growth as the need for security is ever-growing and without the help of the USA, European companies will need to step up and provide more innovation and development to fill this gap.

- ITA (ETF for American based defense assets) - also experienced growth of ~8%, indicating that the defense industry as a whole performed really well over the last 6 weeks. 

- BNP.PA: Experienced growth of ~1% due to increased economically favourable conditions in the EU and overall investor confidence is growing.

- RKT.L: Best performer out of the 4 stocks added in week 3 to help with more exposure to European based assets. Experienced positive growth due to new strategies to increase efficiency for the production aspect in many of its companies that were shown to investors. 

- VICI: unlike PLD, VICI made a small gain. This is mainly due to the difference in business models used by each company. With VICI's model proving to provide more stability during times of extreme volatility.

- EUR and GBP: gains of just over 5% and just under 3% respectively. This shows that the EUR and GBP performed extremely well against the USD, this is due to many factors like investors moving many assets to Europe due to increased uncertainty over American involvement in a potential war, and political and economical uncertainty caused by the government through tariffs and various other legislations and trade deals. 

- BTC: during this period had some severe volatility, but as a long term investment it remains of interest due to possible long term stability, especially with more investment from established businesses and governments. With growth of 3.17%, it shows that BTC may be a reliable investment option. 

- VWO: grew by 4.36% and had relatively low volatility compared to most other assets. Could this be a sign that lower volatility in emerging economies could be due to more stability, especially during the past six weeks having such uncertain turn of events. More growth in recent weeks indicates that emerging markets are beginning to grow more.

- BNDX: as expected it grew along with the inflation rate, which was also <1% a month, giving the portfolio some stable growth and less volatility.


From close on 19th May to close on 3-4th June the portfolio gained a total of 5.05%. 

During this time 

S&P500 Index gained 5.30%

Dow Jones gained 4.76.%

NYSE gained 3.76%

Nikkei 225 gained 6.17%

FTSE gained 1.42%

EUROSTOXX50 dropped by 2.56%


This indicates that the portfolio had a similar performance to the S&P500 and outperformed many markets. This also shows that the Japanese market made significant gains during this time period and outperformed many well performing markets. 


Final Sharpe Ratio :

Sum of weight 0.98 Portfolio                   
Expected return 16.18% 
Portfolio Standard Deviation 11.14%     
Expected Sharp ratio  1.07
